perturbation.original_text: 12.2.3 Any notice given by telefacsimile during the nor mal business hours of the address to the addressee’s domicilium for the time being shall be rebuttably presumed to have been received on the first business day succeeding the day on which the telefacsimile is transmitted.
perturbation.changed_text: 12.2.3 Any notice given by telefacsimile during the nor mal business hours of the address to the addressee’s domicilium for the time being shall be rebuttably presumed to have been received immediately upon successful transmission.
perturbation.explanation: This modification introduces a contradiction about when a telefacsimile notice is considered received. Originally, it was rebuttably presumed to be received on the first business day after transmission. The change states it is rebuttably presumed to be received immediately upon successful transmission. This in-text contradiction creates ambiguity about the effective date of the notice, potentially affecting deadlines and obligations tied to the notice. Depending on whether the transmission was done after business hours, the notice could be effective at two different times, according to the two clauses.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Any notice given by telefacsimile during the nor mal business hours of the address to the addressee’s domicilium for the time being shall be rebuttably presumed to have been received immediately upon successful transmission.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the time when a telefacsimile notice is considered received. The original text stated that notice is rebuttably presumed to be received on the first business day after transmission. The changed text specifies it is rebuttably presumed to be received immediately upon successful transmission. The contradicted text also specifies that notice is rebuttably presumed to be received immediately upon successful transmission. The change from 'first business day succeeding' to 'immediately upon successful transmission' creates a direct contradiction on when the notice is deemed received. This aligns with the 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' perturbation type definition, as it introduces conflicting deadlines regarding the effective date of the notice.

perturbation.original_text: 3.1 The parties acknowledge that the confidential information is a valuable, special and unique asset belonging to the disclosing party and accordingly, that it is of the utmost importance to the disclosing party tha t the confidential information not be used to advance the interest of any persons other than the disclosing party. In view thereof, the parties undertake that -
perturbation.changed_text: 3.1 The parties acknowledge that the confidential information is a valuable asset belonging to the disclosing party. To the extent possible, the confidential information should not be used to advance the interest of any persons other than the disclosing party. In view thereof, the parties will endeavor to undertake that -
perturbation.explanation: This change weakens the obligation to protect confidential information, introducing a contradiction. Initially, the parties acknowledged the 'utmost importance' of not using confidential information to advance other interests and committed to this protection. The modified text changes 'utmost importance' to a general acknowledgment of value and replaces the firm commitment with a weaker 'endeavor to undertake.' This contradicts the original strong obligation, making it less clear how strictly the parties must protect the information. The 'endeavor to undertake' language is vague and allows for possible deviations from the non-disclosure obligation. This weakens the entire purpose of the non-disclosure agreement and creates legal uncertainty regarding the protection of the confidential information, and it directly creates an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The parties acknowledge that the confidential information is a valuable asset belonging to the disclosing party. To the extent possible, the confidential information should not be used to advance the interest of any persons other than the disclosing party. In view thereof, the parties will endeavor to undertake that -
perturbation.justification: The modified text weakens the obligation to protect confidential information, introducing a contradiction as the original text has the 'utmost importance' while the Changed Text replaces 'utmost importance' to a general acknowledgment of value and replaces the firm commitment with a weaker 'endeavor to undertake.' The contradicted section is an identical copy of the Changed Text which indicates the contradiction lies within the text. This weakens the entire purpose of the non-disclosure agreement and creates legal uncertainty regarding the protection of the confidential information, and it directly creates an in-text contradiction.
